# Implement normalization and search the snippet in the hadith dataset
import re, unicodedata

# Load data previously
path = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

snippet_full = "لا يَحِلُّ صَيْدٌ فِي خَمْسٍ: فِي الْحَرَمِ، وَفِي الْحَرِيمِ، وَفِي الْحُرُمِ، وَفِي الْحُرْمَةِ، وَفِي الْحُرْمَةِ الْأُخْرَى"
snippet_short = "لا يَحِلُّ...الْأُخْرَى"

# normalization function
arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""
                             [\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]
                             """, re.VERBOSE)
tatweel = '\u0640'
punctuation = r'''!"#$%&'()*+,\-./:;<=>?@\[\\\]^_`{|}~،؛؟ـ«»“”…ـ—ـ–'"""
# Actually will remove punctuation using regex \W? but careful with Arabic letters.
def normalize_arabic(text):
    # strip tashkeel/diacritics
    text = re.sub(arabic_diacritics, '', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace(tatweel, '')
    # normalize hamza variants: أإآؤئ -> اء? replace to ا for simplicity
    text = re.sub('[أإآ]', 'ا', text)
    text = re.sub('[ؤئ]', 'ء', text)  # preserve hamza on waw/ya as hamza char
    # replace alef maqsura ى -> ي
    text = text.replace('ى', 'ي')
    # remove punctuation and extra spaces
    text = re.sub(r'[' + re.escape('،؟؛:') + r']', ' ', text)
    # remove english punctuation
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\sءاأإآءبتثجحخدذرزسشصضطظعغفقكلمنهويى‌ٰ]', ' ', text) # will remove many things; keep Arabic letters and Latin underscores maybe
    # keep Arabic letters and digits and Arabic-Indic digits
    # Remove Latin letters but pattern may allow underscores; later collapse spaces
    # Remove digits
    text = re.sub(r'[\d۰-۹٠-٩]', '', text)
    # collapse spaces
    text = ' '.join(text.split())
    # lowercase - not applicable for Arabic
    return text

norm_full = normalize_arabic(snippet_full)
norm_short = normalize_arabic(snippet_short)
norm_full, norm_short

import re
with open("/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

def normalize_arabic(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # remove tashkeel/diacritics
    text = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]', '', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640', '')
    # normalize hamza variants to bare alef or hamza
    text = re.sub('[أإآ]', 'ا', text)
    text = re.sub('[ؤئ]', 'ء', text)
    # alef maqsura to ya
    text = text.replace('ى', 'ي')
    # convert to simple Arabic letters only (keep hamza and arabic letters), replace others with space
    text = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF\s]', ' ', text)
    # collapse spaces
    text = ' '.join(text.split())
    return text

snippet_full = "لا يَحِلُّ صَيْدٌ فِي خَمْسٍ: فِي الْحَرَمِ، وَفِي الْحَرِيمِ، وَفِي الْحُرُمِ، وَفِي الْحُرْمَةِ، وَفِي الْحُرْمَةِ الْأُخْرَى"
snippet_short = "لا يَحِلُّ...الْأُخْرَى"

normalize_arabic(snippet_full), normalize_arabic(snippet_short)

def normalize_arabic(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # remove tashkeel/diacritics
    text = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]', '', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640', '')
    # normalize hamza variants to bare alef or hamza
    text = re.sub('[أإآ]', 'ا', text)
    text = re.sub('[ؤئ]', 'ء', text)
    # alef maqsura to ya
    text = text.replace('ى', 'ي')
    # remove Arabic punctuation (comma, semicolon, question)
    text = re.sub(r'[،؛؟]', ' ', text)
    # remove other punctuation marks and latin punctuation
    text = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF\s]', ' ', text)
    # collapse spaces
    text = ' '.join(text.split())
    return text

normalize_arabic(snippet_full)

# Search for candidates containing 'لا يحل' or 'لا يحل صيد' in their normalized texts.
target_terms = ['لا يحل صيد', 'لا يحل', 'لا يحل صيد في خمس', 'لا يحل صيد في خمس في الحرم']
candidates = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):
    text = entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or ''
    norm = normalize_arabic(text)
    for t in target_terms:
        if t in norm:
            candidates.append((idx, entry, text, norm))
            break

len(candidates)

# compute similarity metrics
def token_f1(a, b):
    # a and b are normalized strings
    atoks = a.split()
    btoks = b.split()
    aset = atoks.copy()  # keep duplicates? Better to count overlap by tokens multiset.
    # Use multiset overlap
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(atoks)
    cb = Counter(btoks)
    overlap = sum((ca & cb).values())
    if overlap == 0:
        return 0.0
    prec = overlap / sum(ca.values())
    rec = overlap / sum(cb.values())
    if (prec + rec) == 0:
        return 0.0
    return 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)

def longest_common_substring_ratio(a, b):
    # compute length of longest common substring using dynamic programming (for normalized strings)
    # operate on characters
    if not a or not b:
        return 0.0
    A = a
    B = b
    m = len(A)
    n = len(B)
    # DP for longest common substring
    prev = [0]*(n+1)
    maxlen = 0
    for i in range(1, m+1):
        cur = [0]*(n+1)
        for j in range(1, n+1):
            if A[i-1] == B[j-1]:
                cur[j] = prev[j-1] + 1
                if cur[j] > maxlen:
                    maxlen = cur[j]
            else:
                cur[j] = 0
        prev = cur
    # ratio to average length maybe or to target length (b)
    return maxlen / max(len(A), len(B))
    
# compute metrics for candidates
target_norm = normalize_arabic(snippet_full)
scores = []
for idx, entry, text, norm in candidates:
    f1 = token_f1(norm, target_norm)
    lcs = longest_common_substring_ratio(norm, target_norm)
    scores.append((idx, entry, text, norm, f1, lcs))
# sort by f1 and lcs
sorted_by_f1 = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: x[4], reverse=True)[:30]
sorted_by_lcs = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: x[5], reverse=True)[:30]

sorted_by_f1[:10]

# search for entries with 'صيد' in normalized text
candidates2 = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):
    text = entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or ''
    if 'صيد' in normalize_arabic(text):
        candidates2.append((idx, entry, text, normalize_arabic(text)))
len(candidates2)

scores2 = []
t = normalize_arabic(snippet_full)
for idx, entry, text, norm in candidates2:
    f1 = token_f1(norm, t)
    lcs = longest_common_substring_ratio(norm, t)
    scores2.append((idx, entry, text, norm, f1, lcs))
sorted_scores2 = sorted(scores2, key=lambda x: (x[4], x[5]), reverse=True)[:40]
for s in sorted_scores2[:40]:
    print("idx:", s[0], "f1:", s[4], "lcs:", s[5])
    print(s[1].get('title'))
    print("Matn/hadithTxt snippet:", (s[1].get('Matn') or s[1].get('hadithTxt'))[:200])
    print("-"*80)

toks = normalize_arabic(snippet_full).split()
toks

target_tokens = set(toks)
cands_tokens = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):
    text = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    norm = normalize_arabic(text)
    norm_tokens = set(norm.split())
    # check overlap of target tokens among a certain count
    overlap = target_tokens.intersection(norm_tokens)
    if 'لا' in norm_tokens and 'يحل' in norm_tokens and ('الحرم' in norm_tokens or 'الحرم' in norm or 'الحريم' in norm or 'الحرمه' in norm or 'الحرمة' in norm):
        if len(overlap) >= 4:
            cands_tokens.append((idx, entry, text, norm, overlap))
len(cands_tokens)

cands_tokens = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):
    text = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    norm = normalize_arabic(text)
    norm_tokens = set(norm.split())
    overlap = target_tokens.intersection(norm_tokens)
    if len(overlap) >= 3:

len(cands_tokens)

candidates6 = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):

# compute similarity across all entries for matn/hadithTxt
from collections import defaultdict
target_norm = normalize_arabic(snippet_full)
best = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):
    matn = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    norm = normalize_arabic(matn)
    f1 = token_f1(norm, target_norm)
    lcs = longest_common_substring_ratio(norm, target_norm)

# sort by f1 then lcs

# compute similarity for snippet_short as well
target_norm_short = normalize_arabic(snippet_short)
best_short = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data):

# load quranic_verses.json and maybe see if any verse matches
with open("/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:

# compute similarity for Quranic verses
def get_ayah_text(entry):
    return entry.get('ayah_text') or entry.get('text') or ''

best_q = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(qv):
    ayah = get_ayah_text(entry)

    f1 = token_f1(norm, target_norm)

best_q_sorted = sorted(best_q, key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]), reverse=True)
